According to the learned skills, when far and slightly misaligned (here, almost opposite), moving forward may still yield progress if the environment allows, but since the target is behind, turning is necessary.

However, note: bearing = -178° means the goal is 178° to the left (or equivalently 182° to the right — but we take the smaller angle). Since 178° is almost 180°, it's effectively "behind". But in navigation systems, bearings are typically modulo 360, so -178° ≡ 182°, which is 182° clockwise from current heading — meaning the goal is 178° counterclockwise (i.e., almost directly behind).

text:
The goal bearing is -178°, which is nearly directly behind the agent (since yaw=+0°). The distance is 1339 cm (far). According to navigation skills, when far and misaligned (especially far_left or far_right), MOVE_FORWARD is still preferred over turning if the misalignment isn't extreme—but here, -178° is almost opposite direction.

However, note: bearing = -178° means the goal is 178° to the left of current heading (or equivalently, 182° to the right — but typically bearings are normalized to [-180, 180], so -178° is just 2° short of directly behind).

This suggests the agent is facing almost exactly away from the goal. In such cases, a turn is needed to face the goal. Since -178° is very close to -180° (directly behind), turning either left or right by ~180° would align us. But we can only turn 30° at a time.

text:
The goal bearing is -179°, which is almost directly behind the agent (yaw=+0°). The distance is 1653 cm (far). 

From past experience:
- When behind_right & far: MOVE_FORWARD → regress or no change. Turn first.
- When behind_right & far: TURN_LEFT → neutral (no change) — because it turned *away* from the goal.

Since the bearing is -179° (almost directly behind), and we are at yaw=+0°, the goal is to our rear. To face the goal, we need to turn approximately 180°. Since we can only turn 30° at a time, we should start turning in the direction that reduces the absolute bearing difference.

Bearing = -179° means the goal is 179° to the left of current heading (or 181° to the right — but normalized, it's -179°). So turning LEFT will reduce the magnitude of the negative bearing (e.g., -179° → -149° after one left turn), bringing us closer to 0° alignment.
